workflowList.title: Your Agents
workflowList.agentBuilderDesc: AI generates a workflow from your description
workflowList.blankCanvasDesc: Start from scratch with an empty workflow
workflowList.uploadDragDrop: Drag and drop your Workflow JSON File here, or Click to Select
workflowList.uploadInvalidFile: Please upload a valid JSON file
workflowList.uploadFailed: Failed to upload workflow. Please check if the file is valid.
workflowList.folderDeleteDesc: The {count} agent(s) in this folder won't be deleted - they'll move to Uncategorized.
workflowList.folderEmpty: This folder is empty. Use "Move to folder" on an agent to add it here.
workflowList.defaultPrompt:
# Goal
You are a helpful agent who is handing a conversation over voice with a human. This is a voice conversation, so transcripts can be error prone.

## Rules
- Language: UK English but does not have to be correct english
- Keep responses short and 2-3 sentences max
- If you have to repeat something that you said in your previous two turns, then rephrase a bit while keeping the same meaning. Never repeat the exact same words as in your previous 2 responses.

## Speech Handling
- There could be multiple transcription errors. 
- Accept variations: yes/yeah/yep/aye, no/nah/nope
- If user says "sorry?" or "pardon me" or "can you repeat"  or "what?", they might not have heard you- so just repeat what you just said.

### Flow
Start by saying "Hi". Be polite and courteous.
workflowList.embedDescription: Add "{name}" to any website with a simple script tag.
workflowList.embedEnableDesc: Allow this workflow to be embedded on external websites

workflowList.embedHeadlessInstructions: Add the embed script tag to your page (see below).
workflowList.modelConfigurationDesc: Override global model settings for this workflow. Toggle individual services to customize.

workflowList.templateContextVariablesDesc: Add or remove template context variables that will be available to your workflow. You can use these variables within your workflow nodes within double curly braces. Example: {{variable_name}}.

startCall.name: Start Call
startCall.desc: Entry point of the workflow — plays a greeting and opens the conversation.

agentNode.name: Agent Node
agentNode.desc: Conversational step — the LLM runs one focused exchange.

endCall.name: End Call
endCall.desc: Closes the conversation and hangs up.

globalNode.name: Global Node
globalNode.desc: Persona/tone appended to every agent node's prompt.

trigger.name: API Trigger
trigger.desc: Public HTTP endpoints that launch the workflow.

webhook.name: Webhook
webhook.desc: Send HTTP request after the workflow completes.

startCall.name: Name
startCall.name_desc: Short identifier shown in the canvas and call logs.
startCall.greeting_type_desc: Whether the optional greeting is spoken via TTS from text or played from a pre-recorded audio file.
startCall.greeting_desc: Text spoken via TTS at the start of the call. Supports {{template_variables}}. Leave empty to skip the greeting.
startCall.greeting_placeholder: Hi {{first_name}}, this is Sarah from Acme.
startCall.greeting_recording_id_desc: Pre-recorded audio file played at the start of the call.
startCall.prompt_desc: Agent system prompt for the opening turn. Supports {{template_variables}} from pre-call fetch and the initial context.
startCall.prompt_placeholder: Greet the caller warmly and ask how you can help today.
startCall.allow_interrupt_desc: When true, the user can interrupt the agent mid-utterance.
startCall.add_global_prompt_desc: When true and a Global node exists, prepends the global prompt to this node's prompt at runtime.
startCall.delayed_start_desc: When true, the agent waits before speaking after pickup. Useful for outbound calls where the called party needs a moment to settle.
startCall.delayed_start_duration_desc: Seconds to wait before the agent speaks. 0.1–10.
startCall.extraction_enabled_desc: When true, runs an LLM extraction pass for this node.
startCall.extraction_variables_desc: Each entry declares one variable to capture, with its name, data type, and extraction hint.
startCall.tool_uuids_desc: Tools the agent can invoke during the opening turn.
startCall.pre_call_fetch_enabled_desc: When true, makes a POST request to an external API before the call starts and merges the JSON response into the call context as template variables.
startCall.pre_call_fetch_url_desc: URL the pre-call POST request is sent to. The request body includes caller and called numbers.
startCall.pre_call_fetch_credential_uuid_desc: Optional credential attached to the pre-call request.

agentNode.name: Name
agentNode.name_desc: Short identifier for this step (e.g., 'Qualify Budget'). Appears in call logs and edge transition tools.
agentNode.prompt_desc: Agent system prompt for this step. Supports {{template_variables}} from extraction or pre-call fetch.
agentNode.prompt_placeholder: Ask the caller about their budget and timeline.
agentNode.allow_interrupt_desc: When true, the user can interrupt the agent mid-utterance. Set false for non-interruptible disclosures.
agentNode.add_global_prompt_desc: When true and a Global node exists, prepends the global prompt to this node's prompt at runtime.
agentNode.extraction_enabled_desc: When true, runs an LLM extraction pass for this node.
agentNode.extraction_variables_desc: Each entry declares one variable to capture, with its name, data type, and extraction hint.
agentNode.tool_uuids_desc: Tools the agent can invoke during this step.
agentNode.document_uuids_desc: Documents the agent can reference during this step.

endCall.name: Name
endCall.name_desc: Short identifier shown in call logs. Should describe the ending context (e.g., 'Successful close', 'Polite decline').
endCall.prompt_desc: Agent system prompt for the closing exchange. Supports {{template_variables}} from extraction or pre-call fetch.
endCall.prompt_placeholder: Thank the caller and confirm next steps before ending the call.
endCall.add_global_prompt_desc: When true and a Global node exists, prepends the global prompt to this node's prompt at runtime.
endCall.extraction_enabled_desc: When true, runs an LLM extraction pass before hangup to capture variables from the conversation.
endCall.extraction_prompt_desc: Overall instructions guiding how variables should be extracted from the conversation.
endCall.extraction_variables_desc: Each entry declares one variable to capture from the conversation, with its name, data type, and a per-variable extraction hint.

globalNode.name: Name
globalNode.name_desc: Short identifier shown in the canvas and call logs. Has no runtime effect.
globalNode.prompt_desc: Text appended to every prompted node's system prompt when that node has add_global_prompt=true. Supports {{template_variables}}.
globalNode.prompt_placeholder: You are a friendly assistant calling on behalf of {{company_name}}.

trigger.name: Name
trigger.name_desc: Short identifier shown in the canvas. No runtime effect.
trigger.enabled_desc: When false, the trigger URL returns 404.
trigger.trigger_path_desc: Path segment that uniquely identifies this trigger. Can be customized to a descriptive value up to 36 characters.

webhook.name: Name
webhook.name_desc: Short identifier shown in the canvas and run logs.
webhook.enabled_desc: When false, the webhook is skipped at run time.
webhook.http_method_desc: HTTP verb used for the outbound request.
webhook.endpoint_url_desc: Target URL the webhook request is sent to.
webhook.credential_uuid_desc: Optional credential attached to the webhook request.
webhook.custom_headers_desc: Additional HTTP headers sent with the webhook request.
webhook.payload_template_desc: Jinja-templated JSON body with access to workflow_run_id, initial_context, gathered_context, annotations, and call metadata.

documentUpload.selectFileType: How should the agent use this document?
documentUpload.fullDocumentDesc: The entire document is provided to the agent on each retrieval. Best for menus, price lists, FAQs, and other small reference documents.
documentUpload.chunkedSearchDesc: The document is split into chunks and the most relevant ones are retrieved. Better for large documents like manuals or policies.
documentUpload.invalidType: Please select a supported file type: {formats}
documentUpload.fileTooLarge: File size must be less than 5MB
documentUpload.uploadStorageFailed: Failed to upload file to storage
documentUpload.ossNotice: Uploaded documents are sent to Dograh's managed Model Proxy Service for parsing and chunking. Dograh Model Proxy Service does not store or read your documents - the extracted text and embeddings are returned and stored locally in your self-hosted database.

documentList.deleteConfirm: Are you sure you want to delete "{filename}"?

tester.chatWithAgentDesc: Test the agent over a text conversation. Send messages and see how it responds, with tool calls, transitions, and rewind support.
tester.simulatorDesc: Drive multi-turn, agent-vs-agent tests with a persona prompt.

serviceConfig.apiKeyOverride: API Key (leave empty to use global)
serviceConfig.realtimeModeDesc: Uses a single speech-to-speech model (no separate STT/TTS). An LLM is still required for variable extraction and QA.
